understanding.goal_understanding: Get any pushable object (selected piece or block) to reach or exceed the target_x coordinate to win before exhausting the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement teleports the selected piece 3 units in the chosen direction (UP: dy=-3, RIGHT: dx=+3) without obstruction. No pushing or walls encountered; piece moves freely.

orient_history: 2D plane with 3x3 pixel-unit objects moving in 3-unit steps. MOVE_DIR shifts to *_target if clear, pushes adjacent pushables ('pb') if their target clear (here pb right blocked by immovable wall). Bypass obstacles at y=0 by routing via y=-3 (or +3). Win by sel x>=6 (selected piece) or pb x>=6.

The world is a grid where objects occupy 3x3 pixel cells (positions are top-left corners, multiples of 3). Movements snap by 3 units (one cell) in cardinal directions if unblocked. Pushing occurs when moving into an adjacent pushable object (blocks or controllables) if its target cell is free; walls are immovable blockers. No vertical overlap collision between stacked cells (e.g., y=-3 to 0 doesn't collide with y=0 to 3). Open space above y=0 allows bypassing obstacles to the right. Win by getting any pushable (sel or 'pb') position[0] >= 6. Direct push of 'pb' right blocked by wall; instead, route 'sel' above block to x=6 directly.

World is a pixel-grid (3px=1 cell/step). Controllables ('sel') move 3 units per cardinal direction to *_target unless *_blocked. Blocks ('pb') are pushable when adjacent and targeted via movement. Selected piece or any block reaching x >= target_x wins. No collisions except blocked dirs; down blocked by adjacent 'pb'. Moves from initial [0,0] -> UP [0,-3] -> RIGHT [3,-3].

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_UP | Cannot MOVE_RIGHT (blocked chain). MOVE_UP to [0,-3] (open), then two MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] for sel x=6 win (3 steps total).
history.observe: Initial state: sel at [0,0] size 3x3, pb (pushable block) at [3,0] adjacent right, wt (wall) at [6,0]. right_blocked=true due to pb; up/down/left open.

Win by sel x>=6 (selected piece) or pb x>=6.
history.decide: Cannot MOVE_RIGHT (blocked chain). MOVE_UP to [0,-3] (open), then two MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] for sel x=6 win (3 steps total).

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT to advance 'sel' to [3, -3] (above block, no collision). Next right will reach [6, -3] (x=6, win). Efficient: 2 steps total from here, well under budget.
history.observe: Selected piece moved up from [0, 0] to [0, -3] as a result of the previous MOVE_UP action. No other changes: block 'pb' remains at [3, 0], wall 'wt' at [6, 0]. No adjacencies detected.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT to shift 'sel' to [6, -3], achieving x=6 >= target_x instantly (win). Efficient (1 step); ignores block/wall as path clear/right unblocked.
history.observe: Since last turn (MOVE_RIGHT), selected piece 'sel' advanced from [0, -3] to [3, -3]. Now adjacent_down to pushable block 'pb' at [3, 0]; right_target is [6, -3] with no blockage. Wall 'wt' at [6, 0] is out of path.
